home.title: Miami-Dade Lost & Found Dog Hub
home.subtitle: The community's shared resource for lost and found dogs in Miami-Dade County.
home.lostDesc: Step-by-step protocol to find your dog fast.
home.hubSubtitle: Browse tools, prevention tips, and ways to help.

lost.headline: Let's find your dog. Do these steps in order.
lost.timeEstimate: This checklist takes about 20 minutes to complete.
lost.legalBanner: ⚠️ Miami-Dade County law: The shelter can only hold your dog for 3 days before they become available for adoption. Filing a report today protects your dog.

step1.title: Search your immediate area
step1.body: Walk or drive within 2 blocks of where your dog was last seen. Ask neighbors if they have seen your dog.
step1.why: Most dogs are found within a few blocks of home in the first hour. If these two steps don't lead anywhere, move immediately to Step 2.
checks: Searched my yard and street
Asked 2–3 neighbors

step2.title: Post on these platforms (all free)
step2.tip: ⚠️ Important tip — add your phone number directly in the post description: Both Petco Love Lost and PawBoost hide your contact information from other users and send notifications by email — which frequently land in spam or "Other" folders. Do not rely on platform notifications alone. Put your phone number in the body of every post so finders can reach you directly without waiting on a platform email.
step2.howToPost: How to post — in this order:
checks: Post on Petco Love Lost — AI facial recognition matches your dog's photo against Found reports in real time
Post on PawBoost — auto-distributes to local Facebook pages by area. After posting, follow your local PawBoost Facebook page and turn on post notifications.
Share your Petco Love Lost and PawBoost posts to NextDoor and local Facebook groups — share the existing posts rather than creating new ones
step2.template:
🚨 LOST DOG — [NEIGHBORHOOD] — [DATE]
Name: [Dog name]
Breed/mix: [Description]
Color and markings: [Be specific]
Last seen: [Street or intersection]
Contact: [Your phone number]
[CLEAR PHOTO]

step3.title: File a report with Miami-Dade Animal Services
step3.legalCallout: ⚠️ Filing an official report creates a record. This is your legal protection if your dog ends up at the shelter — and the clock is short. The shelter can only hold your dog for 3 days before they become eligible for adoption.
step3.fosterCallout: 🏠 If a finder has your dog, ask them to keep fostering it at home while you work to reunite. Miami-Dade shelters are at or beyond capacity and resources are stretched. A dog is safer and better cared for in a home than in an intake facility. Finders are legally allowed to keep a found dog while searching for the owner — encourage them to do so.
step3.fileNote: File your report (do this even if you think someone has the dog — it creates an official record):
step3.inPerson: In person: 3599 NW 79 Avenue, Doral FL 33122
step3.hours: Mon–Fri: 10:00 AM–6:30 PM | Sat–Sun: 10:00 AM–4:00 PM | Closed county holidays
checks: Report filed with Animal Services
Looked up my dog's microchip or license tag
Checked the shelter's current listings
step3.redemptionTitle: If your dog is already at the shelter — redemption fees:
step3.redemptionCallout:
Bring your dog's rabies certificate — without it, your dog will not be released until a rabies shot is obtained.
• Sterilized dog: $50 + $10/day boarding
• Unsterilized dog: $100 + $10/day boarding
• Second offense within 12 months: $75–$125 + boarding

step4.title: Expand your search
checks: Make and distribute physical flyers within a 1-mile radius (large photo, phone number in 36pt font minimum, "LOST DOG" bold at top)
Register on Pet Harbor
Post on 24Petconnect
Visit the shelter in person — walk the kennels, ask staff for help

step5.title: Keep going
checks: Check all platforms daily
Check the shelter's listings daily
Update your posts if you have new information
foundChecks: Update all posts: "FOUND — thank you everyone"
Notify Animal Services so your report is closed
Take a photo of the reunion — you earned it

lost.completion: ✅ You've completed all urgent steps. Keep checking daily. Stuck? Call 311 anytime.

found.headline: You did the right thing by stopping. Here's exactly what to do — step by step.
found.timeEstimate: This checklist takes about 30 minutes to complete.
found.legalBanner:
🔴 72-HOUR LEGAL DEADLINE
Miami-Dade County law requires you to:
1. Notify Animal Services, AND
2. Have the dog scanned for a microchip

Both must be done within 72 hours of finding the dog. Failure may result in a civil penalty.
⏱️ Your 72 hours started when you found the dog.
found.phase6Title: Phase 6 — When the Owner Contacts You

step1.title: Check for identification
step1.treeQ1: Does the dog have a collar with a tag?
step1.treeQ2: Is there a phone number on the tag?
step1.treeDone: Call it now. You may be done! ✅
step1.treeQ3: Is there a rabies tag or license number?
step1.treeNote: The Miami-Dade Tag/Microchip Lookup tool can identify the owner from a license or rabies tag number alone.

step2.title: Get the dog scanned for a microchip
step2.legalCallout: ⚠️ Required by Miami-Dade County Ordinance. Must be done within 72 hours of finding the dog.
step2.vet: Any private veterinary clinic — most will scan for free or low cost. Call ahead.
step2.shelter:
Miami-Dade Animal Services — 3599 NW 79 Ave, Doral FL 33122
Mon–Fri: 10:00 AM–6:30 PM | Sat–Sun: 10:00 AM–4:00 PM
step2.chipFound:
⚠️ You must contact the registered owner within 24 hours. This is required by law.
Continue to Step 6 (Owner Verification) as soon as the owner responds.
step2.noChip: No chip found — continue to Step 3.

step3.title: File the official report — and keep the dog with you
step3.legalCallout: ⚠️ Miami-Dade County law requires you to notify Animal Services within 72 hours. Failure to do so may result in a civil penalty.
step3.fosterNote: The goal is to keep this dog out of the shelter. Miami-Dade's shelter system is at or beyond capacity. A dog in a home is safer, calmer, and has a much better chance of being reunited with its owner than a dog in an intake facility. You are doing the right thing by fostering.
step3.howTitle: How to file the official report (do one of these):
checks: Online (preferred): Submit via Petco Love Lost — this form directly notifies Miami-Dade Animal Services. It is the official reporting mechanism.
By phone: Call 311 or 305-468-5900 if you cannot complete the online form
step3.doneNote: That's it for this step. You do not need to go to the shelter.
step3.petcoBtn: File Official Report via Petco Love Lost
step3.deadlineCallout:
⏱️ If you are nearing 72 hours, have not found the owner, and are unable to continue fostering, go to Miami-Dade Animal Services in person to complete a foster application — this keeps the dog legally in your care beyond the 72-hour window while the search continues.
3599 NW 79 Avenue, Doral FL 33122
Mon–Fri: 10:00 AM–6:30 PM | Sat–Sun: 10:00 AM–4:00 PM

step4.title: Post publicly so the owner can find you
step4.wordingTitle: Use this exact wording pattern. Unclear posts slow everything down.
step4.wordingDont: ❌ Do not write: "Lost dog" — that means your dog is missing, which is the opposite
step4.wordingInclude: Include: photo, color, breed or mix, where found, your phone number in the description
step4.wordingExclude: Do not include: your home address
step4.tip: ⚠️ Important tip — add your phone number directly in the post description: Both Petco Love Lost and PawBoost hide your contact information from other users and send notifications by email — which frequently land in spam or "Other" folders. Do not rely on platform notifications alone. Put your phone number in the body of every post so owners can reach you directly without the platform as an intermediary.
step4.howToPost: How to post — in this order:
checks: Post on Petco Love Lost with a clear photo — AI will automatically compare your post against existing lost dog reports
Post on PawBoost — auto-distributes to local Facebook pages by area. After posting, follow your local PawBoost Facebook page and turn on post notifications.
Share your Petco Love Lost and PawBoost posts to NextDoor and local Facebook groups — do not create new separate posts; share the existing ones
Check existing "Lost" reports on Petco Love Lost and PawBoost — does this dog match anyone already searching?

step5.title: Keep the dog safe
step5.legalNote: You are legally allowed to keep the dog in your home while you search for the owner. You do not have to take the dog to the shelter.
bullets: Provide water, food, and a safe, calm space
Keep the dog separated from other pets until temperament is known
If the dog appears injured or ill, call 311 for low-cost vet options
Check your posts daily for responses

step6.title: Verify ownership before releasing the dog
step6.intro: This step protects you, protects the dog, and creates an accountable record. Do not release the dog before completing it.
step6.callout: Anyone can claim a dog. Before you hand the dog over, you need to confirm this is actually their dog. This is standard practice and the owner will understand.
step6.verifyTitle: How to verify — ask for at least two of the following:
checks: Describe the dog's markings without being prompted. Ask the owner: "Can you describe any distinguishing marks, scars, or features?" If they can describe something you didn't mention in your post, that's a strong indicator.
Photos from before the dog went missing. Ask them to show you photos of themselves with the dog on their phone. Look for consistency with the dog in front of you.
Vet records showing the dog's name, breed, and their name as owner.
Microchip registration certificate with their name and the chip number.
Miami-Dade dog license in their name — they can look it up at miamidade.gov/Apps/ASD/TagSearch/ if they have the tag number.
step6.documentTitle: Document the handover — for your own records:
documentChecks: Take a photo of the owner's ID (driver's license or government ID) — ask permission, explain it's for your records
Take a photo of the dog with the owner at the moment of return
Note the date, time, and location of the handover
step6.whyNote: Dog theft is real. Disputes happen. A documented handover protects you legally and ensures the right dog goes to the right person. It also closes the loop on any reports you filed.
afterChecks: Update all posts: "REUNITED — thank you everyone ❤️"
Notify Miami-Dade Animal Services that the dog has been returned to owner

found.completion: ✅ You completed every step — including the legal requirements. You made a difference. Thank you. Questions? Call 311 or 305-468-5900.

resources.title: Resource Directory
resources.subtitle: All tools and organizations that help with lost and found dogs in Miami-Dade County.
resources.facebookNote: Note on Facebook: PawBoost automatically posts to local Facebook groups it has generated by geographic area. This is already organized. Users do not need to manage Facebook groups manually — PawBoost handles it.
resources.capacityNote: A curated list of local rescue organizations with contact information and current capacity status. Most rescues are at or beyond capacity as of 2026.

prevention.title: Prevention
prevention.subtitle: The 3 things that reunite dogs fastest — before they go missing.
prevention.printNote: Designed to be printed as a double-sided card and shared at vet offices, HOA boards, and community centers.
prevention.card1Title: 1. Microchip your dog — and keep the registry current
prevention.card1Body: A chip with an old phone number is useless. The chip is only as good as what's registered to it.
card1Steps: Get chipped (~$25–$45 at most vets; free at county events)
Register the chip immediately (AKC Reunite, HomeAgain, AVID, or 24Petwatch)
Update your phone number every time it changes
prevention.card1Link: Look up your chip or license tag
prevention.card2Body: Tags work even without a scanner. The right finder can call you immediately — no app, no vet required.
card2Tips: Include: dog's name + your cell phone number
Format: embossed directly on collar, or large slide-on tag
Font large enough to read while the dog is moving — not a small dangling disc
Replace when worn, faded, or info changes
prevention.card2Insight: Community insight: Tags printed directly on the collar in large letters are easiest for finders to read on an excited dog.
prevention.card3Title: 3. License your dog in Miami-Dade County
prevention.card3Body: Licensing is required by county law and links your dog to your address and contact info in the county database. A licensed dog can be identified in seconds via the tag lookup tool.
auditItems: Check fence for ground-level gaps — especially after rain, wind, or landscaping
Test gate latches: can they be nosed or pawed open?
Know your dog's preferred escape route and reinforce it
Keep ID tags on even indoor dogs — one open door is all it takes

help.title: Community Help
help.subtitle: You don't have to do everything. Pick ONE thing.
help.reframe: The highest-leverage action for most community members is not donating or fostering — it is replacing your own advice with this protocol. That behavior change scales instantly and costs nothing.
help.card1Body: When you see someone posting "I lost my dog" or "I found a dog" — stop typing your own advice and share this link instead.
help.card1Lost: /lost for someone who lost a dog
help.card1Why: One clear protocol beats 40 conflicting comments. Every time someone shares this link instead of their own advice, the community gets a little more coordinated.
help.card2Body: Give what you can to local rescues covering costs for found animals.
card2Anchors: $49 = one day of emergency boarding for a found stray
help.card2Cta: See vetted local Miami-Dade rescues accepting donations
help.card3Body: Help post flyers when a dog is lost nearby. Scan lost/found posts and flag potential matches. Be the person in your neighborhood group who shares the protocol link.
help.card4Body: Temporarily keep a found dog safe in your home while an owner is located or an adoption is arranged. No experience required. You can specify what you can and can't handle.
help.card5Body: Miami-Dade shelters are at or above capacity. Adoption directly creates space for dogs brought in as found strays.
help.card5Cta: See dogs available now — Pet Harbor Miami-Dade
